title: TextCommandPalette Object
description: Represents the palette that is the Text Command window in Fusion. Remarks

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this palette. Fusion native palettes cannot be deleted. Use the isNative property to determine if this is a native or API created palette.

methods.name: sendInfoToHTML
methods.description: Sends the string to the JavaScript associated with the loaded HTML.

methods.name: setMaximumSize
methods.description: Sets the maximum size of the palette. The user cannot resize it to be larger than this size. This does not change the current size of the palette unless the palette is already larger than this size.   Calling this method and setting the width and height to zero, removes the maximum size restriction.

methods.name: setMinimumSize
methods.description: Sets the minimum size of the palette. The user cannot resize it to be smaller than this size. This does not change the current size of the palette unless the palette is already smaller than this size.   Calling this method and setting the width and height to zero, removes the minimum size restriction.

methods.name: setPosition
methods.description: Sets the position of the palette. If the palette is docked or snapped, this will result in changing it to be floating.

methods.name: setSize
methods.description: Sets the size of the palette. This is best used for a floating palette because either the width or height can be locked when a palette is docked.

methods.name: snapTo
methods.description: Snaps this palette to another palette.

methods.name: writeText
methods.description: Write the specified text to the TEXT COMMAND window.

properties.name: dockingOption
properties.description: Defines the docking behavior for this palette. This controls how the user is allowed to dock the palette.

properties.name: dockingState
properties.description: Gets and sets how the palette is currently docked.

properties.name: height
properties.description: Gets and sets the height of the palette. Setting this property may not always set the height. Depending on how the palette is docked or snapped, the height may not be editable.

properties.name: htmlFileURL
properties.description: Gets and sets the URL to the HTML file that will be displayed in the palette. This can be a local file or a URL on the web where the HTML will be read. To avoid reading a file, this can also be the full HTML definition as a string.   If you are providing the HTML content as a string, it should begin with the  element. Any references made in the HTML should be to URL's and not local files since the local path is ambiguous.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets The unique, language independent, ID of this palette.

properties.name: isNative
properties.description: Indicates if this is one of the standard Fusion palettes or a custom palette created through the API. If true, it is a standard Fusion palette and will have some restrictions on changing its properties and cannot be deleted.

properties.name: isTransparent
properties.description: Returns if this palette was created as a transparent palette.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this palette is currently being displayed in the user interface.

properties.name: left
properties.description: Gets and sets the left side of the palette relative to screen space and in pixels. Because palettes can be positioned outside of the Fusion window, a value of zero indicates the left side of the screen and not the Fusion window.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and set the name of the palette as seen in the user interface. The name of native palettes cannot be set.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: top
properties.description: Gets and sets the top of the palette relative to screen space and in pixels. Because palettes can be positioned outside of the Fusion window, a value of zero indicates the top of the screen and not the Fusion window.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Gets and sets the width of the palette. Setting this property may not always set the width. Depending on how the palette is docked or snapped, the width may not be editable.

events.name: closed
events.description: This event is fired when the user clicks the "Close" button on the palette. You can choose if the "Close" button is available or not when you initially create the palette. When a palette is closed, it still exists but is change to invisible so you can still interact with it and retrieve any needed information and can make it visible again. Use the deleteMe method to delete the palette.

events.name: incomingFromHTML
events.description: This event is fired when the JavaScript associated with the HTML calls the adsk.fusionSendData function. This allows the HTML to communicate with the add-in by passing information to the add-in.

events.name: navigatingURL
events.description: This event is fired when a navigation event occurs on the page. This allows the add-in to determine how this navigation should be handled by the browser.

related_samples.name: Customizing the UI using the API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to work with tabs, panels, and command in the user interface. The full source for C++ and Python samples can be downloaded. This is especially useful for getting the resource files.

classType.title: TextCommandPalette.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.TextCommandPalette.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::TextCommandPalette::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: TextCommandPalette.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this palette. Fusion native palettes cannot be deleted. Use the isNative property to determine if this is a native or API created palette.

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

sendInfoToHTML.title: TextCommandPalette.sendInfoToHTML Method
sendInfoToHTML.description: Sends the string to the JavaScript associated with the loaded HTML. Remarks

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.sendInfoToHTML(action, data)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->sendInfoToHTML(action, data);

sendInfoToHTML.return_description: Returns a string that can be anything that your JavaScript code generates. The JavaScript should always return some content because an empty string is used to indicate a failure.   If useNewWebBrowser flag is set to true while creating the palette control this API call will be asynchronous and an empty string is returned. Response will come in data field of HTMLEvent with action equal to 'response'.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The "action" string to pass to the JavaScript associated with the HTML. This string can be anything but will typically be JSON formatted information.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The "data" string to pass to the JavaScript associated with the HTML. This string can be anything but will typically be JSON formatted information.

setMaximumSize.title: TextCommandPalette.setMaximumSize Method
setMaximumSize.description: Sets the maximum size of the palette. The user cannot resize it to be larger than this size. This does not change the current size of the palette unless the palette is already larger than this size. Calling this method and setting the width and height to zero, removes the maximum size restriction.

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.setMaximumSize(width, height)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->setMaximumSize(width, height);

setMaximumSize.return_description: Returns true if setting the maximum size was succesful.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum width of the palette.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum height of the palette.

setMinimumSize.title: TextCommandPalette.setMinimumSize Method
setMinimumSize.description: Sets the minimum size of the palette. The user cannot resize it to be smaller than this size. This does not change the current size of the palette unless the palette is already smaller than this size. Calling this method and setting the width and height to zero, removes the minimum size restriction.

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.setMinimumSize(width, height)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->setMinimumSize(width, height);

setMinimumSize.return_description: Returns true if setting the minimum size was succesful.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Specifies the minimum width of the palette.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Specifies the minimum height of the palette.

setPosition.title: TextCommandPalette.setPosition Method
setPosition.description: Sets the position of the palette. If the palette is docked or snapped, this will result in changing it to be floating.

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.setPosition(left, top)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->setPosition(left, top);

setPosition.return_description: Returns true if setting the position was successful.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: The position of the left side of the palette relative to screen space and in pixels. Because palettes can be positioned outside of the Fusion window, a value of zero indicates the left side of the screen and not the Fusion window.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: The position of the top of the palette relative to screen space and in pixels. Because palettes can be positioned outside of the Fusion window, a value of zero indicates the top of the screen and not the Fusion window.

setSize.title: TextCommandPalette.setSize Method
setSize.description: Sets the size of the palette. This is best used for a floating palette because either the width or height can be locked when a palette is docked.

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.setSize(width, height)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->setSize(width, height);

setSize.return_description: Returns true if the sizing was succesful. It is still considered a success even if the width or height could not be changed because of how the palette is docked or snapped.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Specifies the width of the palette. Depending on how the palette is docked or snapped, the width may not be editable.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Specifies the height of the palette. Depending on how the palette is docked or snapped, the height may not be editable.

snapTo.title: TextCommandPalette.snapTo Method
snapTo.description: Snaps this palette to another palette.

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.snapTo(palette, snapOption)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->snapTo(palette, snapOption);

snapTo.return_description: Returns true if the palette was successfully snapped to the other palette.

parameters.name: palette
parameters.description: Specifies the palette to snap to.

parameters.name: snapOption
parameters.description: Specifies how this palette should be snapped to the other palette.

writeText.title: TextCommandPalette.writeText Method
writeText.description: Write the specified text to the TEXT COMMAND window. Remarks

syntax.python: returnValue = textCommandPalette_var.writeText(text)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = textCommandPalette_var->writeText(text);

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to write to the Text Command window.

closed.title: TextCommandPalette.closed Event
closed.description: This event is fired when the user clicks the "Close" button on the palette. You can choose if the "Close" button is available or not when you initially create the palette. When a palette is closed, it still exists but is change to invisible so you can still interact with it and retrieve any needed information and can make it visible again. Use the deleteMe method to delete the palette.

navigatingURL.title: TextCommandPalette.navigatingURL Event
navigatingURL.description: This event is fired when a navigation event occurs on the page. This allows the add-in to determine how this navigation should be handled by the browser.
